name: Ourense – city centre tour
name_original: Ourense – vuelta del centro de la ciudad
region_intro: No traffic touring cycling routes around the Mio region are characterized by a diverse landscape, featuring river valleys and extensive forest areas. The terrain offers a mix of flat sections along waterways and rolling hills, providing varied cycling experiences. This area is known for its natural beauty, with routes often paralleling rivers and winding through dense woodlands. Touring cyclists can expect to encounter a range of surfaces suitable for different cycling preferences. Best no traffic touring cycling routes around the Mio The most popular no traffic touring cycling route is Miño River and Lugo City Loop, a 9.2 miles (14.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 3 minutes to complete. This route features gentle elevation changes along the river and through urban areas. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Outeiro de Rei – return via A Feira de Monte and Torre de Caldaloba, a moderate 28.7 miles (46.2 km) path. This route offers a mix of open countryside and historical points of interest. Local touring cyclists also love the Meira – tour around Riotorto, a 26.9 miles (43.4 km) trail leading through varied rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Touring cycling around the Mio is defined by river paths, rolling hills, and forested sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Mio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1,400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic touring cycling routes are available around Mio?
A: There are over 75 dedicated no-traffic touring cycling routes around Mio, offering a wide range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars.

Q: What types of terrain and surfaces can I expect on these routes?
A: The routes around Mio offer a diverse mix of terrain. While the region is known for its picturesque AuSable River Valley and Huron National Forest, you'll find everything from paved sections to crushed stone paths, and even some more challenging gravel roads and two-tracks. The area's varied landscape includes rolling hills, especially in the northern sections, providing a dynamic cycling experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly no-traffic cycling routes in Mio?
A: Yes, several routes are suitable for families. While the guide focuses on touring, many of the less challenging routes, particularly those on paved or well-maintained crushed stone surfaces, are great for family outings. Look for routes with lower elevation gain and shorter distances for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What are some notable natural features or scenic points I can experience while cycling?
A: Cycling around Mio offers stunning natural beauty. Many routes parallel the iconic AuSable River, providing captivating riverside vistas and a tranquil atmosphere. You'll also ride through the expansive Huron National Forest, experiencing towering pines and diverse woodlands. The region is known for its vibrant fall colors and serene winter landscapes, offering beautiful views year-round.

Q: Are there any specific trails or areas known for being traffic-free?
A: Yes, the region offers several excellent options for traffic-free cycling. The **North Central State Trail** and the **North Eastern State Trail** are both extensive crushed stone trails, ideal for gravel touring cyclists seeking varied scenery and wildlife viewing. Additionally, the **Grayling Bicycle Turnpike** offers a 6.5-mile asphalt and gravel route through forested landscapes.

Q: What kind of attractions can I visit along the no-traffic cycling routes?
A: While cycling, you can explore various historical and natural attractions. You might encounter significant landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/751631" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portomarín Bridge&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3157769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte Vella (Roman Bridge of Ourense)&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in history, the ancient city of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lugo&lt;/a&gt; with its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/334304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint Mary&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight. Natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2681874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belesar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5338823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbantes Thermal Park&lt;/a&gt; also offer scenic stops.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Mio?
A: The best time to cycle in Mio largely depends on your preference. Spring and fall offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with fall being particularly stunning due to the vibrant foliage. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer. Winter cycling is possible for those prepared for colder conditions and snow, offering a unique, serene experience.

Q: Are there any circular no-traffic touring routes available?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes designed for touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017353176/mino-river-and-lugo-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño River and Lugo City Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 14.8 km ride, while the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Rato Bike Path – Roman Bridge over the Miño River loop from Lugo&lt;/a&gt; spans over 51 km.

Q: Where can I find parking for these no-traffic bike trails?
A: Parking is generally available at trailheads and designated access points for the larger trail networks like the North Central State Trail and the North Eastern State Trail. Many state parks and forest campgrounds in the Huron National Forest also offer parking facilities that can serve as starting points for your rides.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly no-traffic cycling routes?
A: Many multi-use trails in the Huron National Forest and surrounding areas are dog-friendly, provided your dog is on a leash and you adhere to Leave No Trace principles. Always check specific trail regulations before heading out, as rules can vary. The natural surface trails are generally more accommodating for dogs than paved paths.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the no-traffic touring routes around Mio?
A: The routes around Mio cater to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with 9 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 56 difficult routes. This means whether you're looking for a leisurely ride or a challenging adventure with significant elevation changes, there's an option for you.

Q: Can I find cafes or accommodation near the no-traffic cycling routes in Mio?
A: Mio and its surrounding towns offer various amenities for cyclists. You can find local cafes and pubs in Mio itself, perfect for a break. For overnight stays, there are accommodations ranging from hotels and motels to campgrounds within the Huron National Forest, many of which are conveniently located near trail access points.
tips:
The Roman bridge of Ourense known as Ponte Vella or Puente Mayor, over the Miño river, was built in the 1st century AD. C. within a branch of Via XVIII or Via Nova.
According to tradition, it dates from the time of Trajan, however its construction characteristics bring it closer to the time of Augustus. Of that first bridge, only a few padded ashlars from the bases remain, as mute witnesses of the past.

The thermal springs of As Burgas are one of the most emblematic and beloved spaces in the city, directly linked to its origins. This is where the ancient Aquis Aurienses ("golden waters") would be born, a Roman settlement formed around these mineral-medicinal springs that today continue to flow at more than 60° C in the heart of the Historic Center.
Center and heart of monumental Ourense, its sloping floor is striking, a real rarity among the main squares in Spain.
